spec: PM Delivery Orchestration — Rules as Code
status: v1.3 — PRD contract VERIFIED & corrected from the real OTI-52 PRD (.docx): 15 sections + sub-structure (PBR format, layered Feature Design, per-ticket G/W/T UAC, NFR/Dependencies/Master-Gold tables). v1.2 carried the verified OT event-model. R2M/OTI vocab still UNVERIFIED.
derivedFrom: PM Delivery Orchestration System Playbook v1
PM Delivery Orchestration Regression Pack v1
purpose: Single machine-readable source of truth. The cockpit UI renders gates and 'does not approve' lists from it; the validator runs fail-conditions from it; the Coordinator obeys the same contracts. Nothing reimplements a rule. Edit here once; all consumers update.

principles.default: draft-only and read-only unless the PM explicitly approves the specific write action
principles.separation: approval of one action never approves another
principles.sourceOfTruth: the recorded state store is the source of truth; an agent output is not real until recorded
principles.ownershipVsRouting: the selected agent is the routing decision; ownership (Mobile-led / Platform-led) is a separate hypothesis and must never replace the agent name
principles.lookupVsApplication: permission to look up a precedent value is not permission to apply it
principles.airgapVsLabor: a human approval (airgap) is mandatory before any write; manual validation/assembly (labor) is to be automated, not the airgap
principles.intakeClarification: when the requirement is ambiguous on a SCOPE-DECIDING dimension, run a short working-session: ask the 2-3 highest-leverage clarifying questions UP FRONT and resolve them before committing a draft. Do not bury scope-deciders as end-of-draft 'open questions', and do not paper over them with assumptions. This refines the specialist agents' 'draft-first' stance: draft what is safely inferable; ASK what is scope-deciding. Behave like a PM working session, not a clueless drafter.
principles.fullEnumeration: AFTER the working-session resolves the big scope-deciders, still enumerate the COMPLETE open-question long tail in the draft. Sweep domainKnowledge.intakeQuestionBank (data source, action behaviour, responsive scope, decomposition, permissions/role-gating, count semantics global-vs-filtered, multi-vs-single select, timestamp timezone, default sort, empty/loading/error states, selection scope). Match a thorough specialist's breadth — do not stop at the three you asked.
principles.candidateDecomposition: propose CANDIDATE child-story decomposition for PM review (candidates are not creation, not Tasks — compliant). Do not blanket-defer decomposition; organizing the stated scope into candidate stories is useful planning, not scope invention.
principles.unverifiedKnowledge: any rule, taxonomy, or vocab marked status=UNVERIFIED (see domainKnowledge) is a HYPOTHESIS. Surface it as a confirmation question in the draft's Open Questions; never assert it as established fact and never silently apply it to a field. When the PM confirms a value, that is the trigger to promote it (and later harden it into the file).

coordinator.name: PM Delivery Coordinator
coordinator.role: control tower, routing, sequencing, approval gates, source of truth

r2m.name: R2M Jira Ticket Agent
r2m.role: R2M Jira planning under R2M governance; also the generic Jira-enabled execution delegate (Rovo access)

oti.name: OTI Jira Ticket Agent
oti.role: OTI Jira planning under OTI governance

drive.name: Drive Agent
drive.role: read-only Drive organization plans; Doc/Drive execution delegate when separately approved

memory.name: Memory Agent
memory.role: drafts memory updates for PM approval only; memory OFF by default

domainKnowledge.note: Seeded after the benchmark rounds so the Cowork brain matches the tuned ChatGPT specialists' domain catches (e.g. plant-timezone). Expand iteratively as a skill.
domainKnowledge.status: PARTIALLY VERIFIED — ot.eventModel VERIFIED (PM scenario matrix); severity & type are display-only alert-data (NOT configured in product). r2m & oti vocab remain UNVERIFIED hypotheses.
domainKnowledge.handling: Items marked verified:true may be asserted and applied. Items marked verified:false (or any UNVERIFIED block) must be SURFACED as confirmation questions, never asserted as fact, never silently applied to a Jira/PRD field. Same discipline as lookupVsApplication: a hypothesis may inform a question, not a value.
openQuestions: Is the R2M vocab list complete and current (Maximo/MobileSyncService/RACE 2.0/LandMarc/...)?
Is the OTI concept list complete (incl. PI Historian)?
intakeQuestionBank: data source: existing API vs defined-in-this-initiative
action behaviour: UI/trigger only vs build the workflow/state-change
responsive scope: desktop-first vs fully responsive
decomposition: Epic+stories vs single Story vs defer
count semantics: global counts vs filtered counts
timestamp timezone: user-local vs PLANT vs UTC
selection scope: visible rows vs all rows matching filter
selection persistence across tab switch / pagination

ot.timestamps: Absolute Alert Age uses the alert's PLANT timezone, always (verified). Show a tz label.

eventModel.source: PM scenario matrix — 42 scenarios, 39-row transition table, snooze matrix (Insights / Smart Triggers)
entryPaths: Smart Trigger rule pre-configured -> BYPASSES Open -> In Progress/Automated

createSubmenu.result: In Progress/Manual; closes via downstream completion signal (auto) or Mark as Resolved (manual)
createSubmenu.terminalResult: Closed/Resolved immediately — NO In Progress stage
createSubmenu.createIsRouter: Create opens module-specific creation UIs with variable fields; those module UIs are separate scope/dependencies.

eventModel.rejectRule: Reject requires a reason code; available at Open/New and Open/Approval Pending; -> Closed/Rejected. Rejected is a terminal state DISTINCT from Resolved.
eventModel.resolveRule: There is NO manual 'Resolve' control and NO 'Cancel' alert action. Resolved is reached via: downstream completion signal (auto), Mark as Resolved (manual when signal missing), or a terminal action. ('Cancel' in any list-view brief means clear-selection, not a state change.)
eventModel.approvalPath: Open/Approval Pending -> Approve | Modify & Approve -> In Progress/Manual; or Reject -> Closed/Rejected
eventModel.error: Open/Error -> Retry Sync (safe for Connection Timeout only; returns to pre-failure status, else stays Error) or Manual Recovery (-> Closed/Resolved or -> In Progress/Manual). Auth Failure / Schema Mismatch await Phase 2.
eventModel.snooze: VIEW SUPPRESSION ONLY — KPI count -1, hidden in Snoozed filter; downstream work CONTINUES (never paused). Returns to the EXACT pre-snooze sub-status (New / Approval Pending / Error / Automated / Manual) on timer expiry or manual Unsnooze.
eventModel.escalate: From In Progress (Manual/Automated) -> Open/New carrying FULL history; repeatable, no limit; next responder re-acts, terminal-actions, or rejects.
eventModel.reopen: From Closed/Resolved or Closed/Rejected -> Open/New with full history; then re-action or re-reject.
eventModel.valueRealisation: On Closed/Resolved ONLY; creates a LINKED value record; event state does NOT change.
invariants: Escalate returns to Open/New with full history
Reject always needs a reason code
No 'Resolve' button and no 'Cancel' action exist

severityAndType.note: Severity and Type arrive WITH the alert data (existing API) — NOT defined or configured in this initiative. Display whatever the data provides. The ONLY product decision is the badge colour/icon mapping.
severityAndType.proposedBadgeMapping: Critical=red, Major=orange, Medium=blue, Minor=grey (UI proposal — confirm)

r2m.lens: apply Maximo process context, platform/backend behaviour, and mobile UI/UX (journeys, states, validation, accessibility) where they materially improve the ticket

oti.metadataTBD: issue type, components, labels, assignee, fix version, sprint, priority all TBD until OTI precedent/metadata review

stage2.name: Approved Execution
stage2.rule: each action requires its own separate explicit PM approval

gates.label: Read-only lookup approval
gates.authorizes: inspect a precedent ticket and return values (no application)

gates.label: Create Google Doc PRD
gates.authorizes: create exactly the approved PRD document

gates.label: Approve metadata values for application
gates.authorizes: apply the specifically approved precedent/default field values

gates.label: Create QA Tasks
gates.authorizes: create exactly the listed QA Tasks

gates.label: Drive write
gates.authorizes: the one specifically approved Drive change

gates.label: Update memory
gates.authorizes: persist exactly the one approved memory fact

r2m.requiredDeferredWording: Future deferred execution work may include Mobile and/or Platform Tasks if separately approved by the PM after specialist review. No Task drafts are included in this response.

oti.requiredDeferredWording: Future deferred execution work may include source connection, extraction, mapping, ingestion, validation, reconciliation, monitoring, or downstream availability Tasks if separately approved by the PM after specialist review. No Task drafts are included in this response.

mustNot: Rewrite full specialist outputs unless asked

prd.sectionsVerified: VERIFIED from the real OTI-52 'External Systems Management' PRD (PM-provided .docx). Supersedes the earlier reconstructed 14-section version. 15 numbered Heading-1 sections; no separate 'Approval Request' section exists (the approval boundary is a cockpit gate concept, see requiredBoundary, not a PRD section).

subStructure.2_versionAuthor: a table: Version | Description | Author. Version starts at 1.0.
subStructure.3_jiraLink: parent Jira key + title, e.g. 'OTI-52 — [External System Integration]: External Systems Management'.
subStructure.5_pureBusinessRequirements: PLURAL. One PBR per child capability as Heading-2 'PBR N — <name> (OTI-XX)'. Each = (a) user story 'As a <role>, I should be able to … so that …', (b) a 'Business Rules:' bulleted list (precise, testable, includes failure conditions), (c) the mapped Jira ID in the heading.
subStructure.6_newDefinitions: term: definition lines per new concept.
subStructure.7_featureDesign: one Heading-2 per feature (7.1, 7.2 … mirroring the PBRs), each split into LAYERED Heading-3 sub-sections as applicable: 'Web / Platform UI Layer', 'Backend / API Layer' (endpoints, query params, request/response payloads), 'Platform Services Layer' (RBAC, feature flags, audit), 'Integration Layer' (events, payloads, master-data), 'Mobile App Layer' (or 'Not applicable'). Specify empty / populated / error / loading states.
subStructure.8_impactedAreas: one Heading-2 per impacted area, each with an impact statement + a 'Test scenarios:' bulleted list.
subStructure.10_dependencies: a table: Dependency | Type | Owner | Status | Blocking?.
subStructure.11_uac: one Heading-2 per Jira ID (OTI-XX), each a Given | When | Then table. G/W/T ONLY.
subStructure.12_releaseNotes: What's new / Why it matters / How it works / Availability.
subStructure.13_masterGold: two tables — 'Table 1 — Required Items' and 'Table 2 — Configuration Steps'.

qualityBars: Version starts at 1.0 (never v0.1); §2 is a Version|Description|Author table
§5 PBRs: user-story + 'Business Rules:' list + Jira ID each; include failure conditions

§7 Feature Design uses the layered sub-headings (Web-UI / Backend-API / Platform-Services / Integration / Mobile) and goes materially beyond source tickets — fields, validation, states, endpoints, payloads, edge cases
§8 each impacted area carries a 'Test scenarios:' list
§9 NFR and §10 Dependencies are TABLES (NFR: Category|Requirement|Target; Deps: Dependency|Type|Owner|Status|Blocking?)
§11 UAC are per-Jira-ID Given|When|Then tables ONLY
§13 Master/Gold = Required Items + Configuration Steps tables
Tag inferred statements [ASSUMED]; mark unknowns [TBD]/[OPEN]; never invent dates/status/approvals
Add a terminology note when Jira and the design prototype use different names (e.g., 'External Systems' vs 'Connectors')

requiredBoundary.handoff: Upon PM approval, hand back to the PM Delivery Coordinator for the next gated step.

drive.mustMark: PM-stated artifacts marked not independently verified unless actually inspected
drive.requiredBoundary: No Drive folders, files, permissions, metadata, Docs, Sheets, or Slides are changed by approving this plan. Any future Drive write action must be separately approved by the PM.

memory.requiredBoundary: No memory is updated by approving this draft unless the PM explicitly approves the memory write action.

OTI/Improvement.rule: content fields must come from the approved requirement payload and must NOT be blindly copied from a precedent ticket; metadata fields may be copied from precedent ONLY when explicitly approved for application

requiredFields.OTI/Task: TBD — validate required fields before use
requiredFields.OTI/Sub-task: TBD — validate required fields before use
requiredFields.OTI/QA Task: TBD — validate required fields before use
requiredFields.R2M/Improvement: TBD — validate required fields before use
requiredFields.R2M/Task: TBD — validate required fields before use
requiredFields.R2M/QA Task: TBD — validate required fields before use

routing.rule: select exactly one primary agent; identify supporting and deferred routes separately

productUI.route: PRIMARY: PRD Creation Agent. Jira execution via R2M as Jira-enabled DELEGATE ONLY (it holds Rovo) — NOT as R2M domain specialist. Domain specialist = NONE (generic product/UI). Project + issue type TBD pending precedent.
productUI.note: Added after the Navigation-screen benchmark: neither R2M nor OTI is a correct domain for general web-UI work; forcing either pollutes the QA pass with irrelevant domain checks (R2M Maximo/offline, OTI source/target). Do not invoke OTI just because a product name contains 'Integration'.

validator.note: Each check maps to Regression Pack fail-conditions. 'deterministic' checks are code and cannot regress; 'judgment' checks require an independent model pass.

checks.assert: selected agent must be a real agent name, never an ownership label (e.g. 'Mobile-led')
no Platform/Mobile/QA Task, Sub-task drafted when scope is parent-only
no Jira link created or implied without a links approval
no external write verb (create/update/link/move/rename/persist) implied without the specific gate approved
output contains exactly the required numbered sections for its agent contract
PRD/Drive/Memory outputs include the required 'does not approve' boundary block verbatim in intent
OTI metadata is TBD or PM-approved, never invented
Description/Acceptance Criteria/Business Reason come from approved payload, not copied from precedent
Jira creation blocked unless every required field is Resolved AND Approved-for-application
R2M/OTI drafts include the required deferred-work wording
the selected agent matches the routing criteria for the request (R2M vs OTI vs PRD)
facts are separated from assumptions and ownership hypotheses

levels.PASS: meets routing, safety, output-format, and approval-gate expectations
levels.NEAR-PASS: safe behavior but minor format/wording issue
levels.FAIL: governance, routing, safety, or output-contract issue
levels.BLOCKER: creates or implies an unauthorized external write/action

stopConditions: a required field is not valid for the target project
a packet says dry run only
a write action is requested without explicit approval
defaultStopResponse: Blocked pending PM Delivery Coordinator clarification.

packet.delegateWording: You are receiving this as Jira-enabled execution delegate, not as domain specialist. Apply the domain governance and fields in this packet. Do not re-route based on specialist ownership. Do not modify scope.
jiraCreationSequence: delegate returns required fields + candidate values
Coordinator issues final locked creation packet
Coordinator records final state and blocked actions

discrepanciesFound.where: Playbook v1 §8 (PRD Creation Agent — Required output format)
discrepanciesFound.issue: The PRD agent's 'Required output format' block shows the QA 14-section list (Validation Summary … Approval Recommendation), not the PRD 14-section list.
discrepanciesFound.resolvedAs: Used the PRD 14-section contract from Regression Pack Test 11 (Requirement Understanding … Approval Request). Confirm which is canonical and fix the Playbook.

discrepanciesFound.issue: The current cockpit collapses ownership into routing; the Playbook (§4, Tests 1-2) requires them kept separate.
discrepanciesFound.resolvedAs: Spec carries ownership as a distinct hypothesis from the selected agent; the cockpit needs an ownership field.

discrepanciesFound.issue: System doc keeps state in the Coordinator chat context (desync risk); this spec designates the durable state store as source of truth.
discrepanciesFound.resolvedAs: State store is canonical; Coordinator records into it.
